# Luiz Carlos da Silva Junior — Portfolio

> XR Engineer with 10+ years of experience architecting real-time 3D systems in Unity for industrial, medical, and training applications.
> Founder and CTO of FormulaXR, delivering production XR platforms to companies including Embraer, Nestlé, and Becton Dickinson.

Website: https://luizcarlosfx.github.io
Email: luizcarlos.sfx@gmail.com
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/luizcarloxr/
GitHub: https://github.com/luizcarlosfx
Discord: luizcarlosfx

Specialized in cross-platform XR architecture (Quest, mobile, PC, WebGL), real-time networking, and performance optimization for constrained devices. Available for remote work.

---

## Resume

### Experience

#### Founder & CTO — FormulaXR (2015 – Present)

- Architected XRProj, a multi-platform XR content creation system (VR, AR, mobile, PC, WebGL) adopted by Embraer, Nestlé, Ball Corporation, and Aegea
- Delivered VR surgical training simulations for Becton Dickinson, Ortosíntese, and Hospital Beneficência Portuguesa, enabling scalable training with LMS integration and step-by-step evaluation systems
- Built and shipped XRTracker, a commercial Unity plugin for markerless 6DOF object tracking using custom computer vision
- Migrated a production multiplayer medical VR platform from Photon PUN to FishNet, improving scalability and reducing hosting costs
- Led cross-functional teams across full project lifecycles, from client discovery and technical scoping through architecture, development, and delivery

Tech stack: Unity, C#, Photon PUN, FishNet, ASP.NET Core, WebGL, Quest, HoloLens, Android, iOS

### Technical Skills

- **XR Development**: VR / AR / MR (Unity — primary, Unreal Engine)
- **Programming**: C#, C++, JavaScript, TypeScript, Java
- **Networking**: Photon PUN, FishNet, Netcode for GameObjects, real-time multiplayer architectures
- **Real-Time Systems**: Interaction systems, simulation, object tracking, computer vision
- **Performance**: Optimization for mobile VR (Quest, Android, iOS)
- **Backend**: ASP.NET Core
- **Platforms**: Quest, PC, Mobile, WebGL

### Game Development

- Implemented real-time multiplayer networking for a mobile game with 1M+ downloads and a 4.6 rating
- Developed a VR basketball experience using Unreal Engine for Meta Quest
- Developed gameplay systems using Unreal Engine's Gameplay Ability System (GAS)
- Currently developing an independent title for release in 2026

### Education

**B.Sc. Computer Science — IBTA** (2011 – 2015)
Instituto Brasileiro de Tecnologia Avançada

### Languages

- Portuguese: Native
- English: Conversational

---

## Industry & Simulation Projects

### XRProj

**XRProj** is a production multi-platform XR content creation system that enables teams to bring CAD projects into VR (PC and Quest), AR (Android and iOS), HoloLens, and WebGL — all from a single unified pipeline.

As **Lead Developer and Architect**, I drove this project from initial concept through nearly four years of continuous development, building every major system including real-time networking, serialization, and cross-platform deployment.

Adopted by **Embraer, Nestlé, Ball Corporation, and Aegea** for CAD visualization, assembly validation, and collaborative design review.

Key contributions:

- Architected the full project from concept to production
- Performance optimization for constrained platforms (Quest, Android, iOS)
- Built native CAD-to-XR model conversion pipeline (CAD and polygon formats)
- Implemented real-time multi-user system using Photon Unity Networking
- Developed and integrated backend services (ASP.NET Core)
- Led cross-functional teams across full project lifecycle

Learn more at xrproj.com (https://www.xrproj.com)

---

### XRTracker

**XRTracker** is a markerless 3D object tracking SDK for Unity that delivers **6DOF pose estimation with sub-centimeter precision** — no scanning, markers, or cloud training required.

Built from the ground up with a **native C++ computer vision core and GPU-accelerated rendering**, it achieves 4–8ms per frame on mobile devices. Three tracking algorithms — silhouette, edge, and depth — can be used individually or combined for maximum robustness, even in low-light and low-contrast industrial environments.

Key features:

- Markerless model-based tracking with three composable algorithms (silhouette, edge, depth)
- No-code setup — fully configurable through the Unity Inspector
- Tracking model generation from any 3D mesh (FBX, OBJ, glTF) directly in the editor
- Cross-platform: Windows, iOS (ARKit), Android (ARCore)
- Designed for Industry 4.0: Poka-Yoke workflows, assembly guidance, and task validation

Visit xrtracker.net (https://xrtracker.net) or read the documentation (https://docs.xrtracker.net)

---

### Virtua — Medical VR Training

**Virtua** is a VR medical training platform developed for **Becton Dickinson (BD)**, a multinational healthcare company. The application trains healthcare professionals on catheter system procedures, and doubles as a sales demonstration tool for hospital procurement teams.

A key innovation was the **artist-friendly task authoring system** — a step-by-step procedure builder that allows medical content creators to define training sequences without any programming knowledge.

Key contributions:

- Designed and built an extensible task system for step-by-step medical procedures
- Developed a grading and evaluation system with configurable action assessments
- Integrated with BD's Learning Management System (LMS) for tracking and certification
- Built runtime procedure loading via Asset Bundles for content scalability

---

### FormulaXR — Educational Portal

A **full-stack educational platform** built for **FormulaXR** that hosts 40+ interactive science simulations powered by Unity. Students explore subjects like Biology, Chemistry, and Physics through hands-on 3D experiences — from food chain ecosystems to interactive periodic tables.

I developed the **entire backend and frontend** using ASP.NET Core and Blazor WebAssembly, as well as the **core Unity package** used to produce all simulations. A custom communication layer bridges Unity WebGL builds and the portal, tracking student progress, evaluating actions, and reporting results in real time.

Key contributions:

- Full-stack development of the web portal (ASP.NET Core + Blazor WebAssembly)
- Architected the core Unity simulation package used across all 40+ simulations
- Built the communication bridge between Unity WebGL and the portal for real-time progress tracking and evaluation
- Student action tracking, grading, and reporting system

Visit formulaxr.com (https://formulaxr.com)

---

### OrtoVR — Surgical Training

**OrtoVR** is a VR training application developed for **Ortosíntese**, a Brazilian orthopedic company. The software instructs surgeons on the proper use of prosthetic tools and was featured as a key demonstration at a major healthcare fair in 2018.

Key contributions:

- Architected the full project structure
- Designed and implemented a VR task system for guided surgical procedures
- Refined a custom VR interaction system for precise instrument handling
- Integrated network code for collaborative training sessions

---

### VR Surgery — Hospital BP

A **proof-of-concept** developed for **Hospital Beneficência Portuguesa de São Paulo (BP)** — one of Brazil's most prestigious healthcare institutions, recognized among the world's best hospitals for seven consecutive years. The project simulates surgical procedures using a robotic arm controlled through a VR headset, exploring the intersection of hardware integration and immersive simulation.

Key contributions:

- Integrated hardware drivers for robotic arm control
- Developed the VR interface and UI for surgical interaction
- Refactored and stabilized the existing codebase

---

### Virtual Classroom

A **multi-user virtual classroom** developed in collaboration with the City Hall of São José dos Campos, Brazil. This project laid the foundation for several core systems I continue to use across all my VR projects today.

Key contributions:

- Built an extensible VR interaction system (teleportation, object manipulation) reused across subsequent projects
- Developed a modular VR networking layer designed for easy extension
- Performance optimization for smooth multi-user experiences

---

### Forklift Safety Training

A **VR forklift training simulator** developed for **Nestlé**. The application walks operators through critical safety checkpoints — pre-operation inspection, environment awareness, load handling — and evaluates whether each step is performed correctly in real time.

Custom-built in **Unity for Meta Quest**.

Key contributions:

- Full development of the VR training simulator
- Real-time evaluation system for safety checkpoints
- Forklift physics and controls tuned for VR
- Performance optimization for Meta Quest

---

### Industrial Painter Simulator

A **multi-user VR simulation** of an industrial painting machine, developed for a major multinational corporation. The application supports both **VR and desktop modes** within the same shared environment, allowing teams to collaborate regardless of hardware.

Key contributions:

- Full development of the simulation codebase
- Multi-user networking with simultaneous VR and desktop participants
- Layer system and animation logic for the painting machine
- Cross-platform experience (VR headset + non-VR desktop)

---

### DuPont — VR Product Tour

A **VR city tour experience** developed for **DuPont**'s internal event **"Next Generation DuPont"**, which ran over 3 consecutive days and was experienced by **800+ attendees**. Built using the Oculus DK2, the application guides users through an interactive virtual city, highlighting DuPont products integrated into everyday environments — showcasing the company's presence in daily life.

Key contributions:

- Full development of the VR experience
- Interactive city environment with product discovery points
- Built for Oculus DK2 — one of the earliest commercial VR development kits

---

### Climatempo — VR Experience

A **VR experience** developed for **Climatempo (https://www.climatempo.com.br/)** and **Kersys** for the **RM Vale 2016** event. The application takes users through interactive scenes showcasing weather and environmental data in an immersive format.

Key contributions:

- Full development of the VR application
- Scene navigation and transition system

---

## Games & Tools

### Truco Animado

**Truco Animado** is a multiplayer mobile card game based on Truco, one of Brazil's most popular card games. With **over 1 million downloads** on Google Play and a **4.6/5 star rating** across 23,000+ reviews, it's one of the top Truco games on the platform.

Brought in to implement multiplayer, I discovered the existing codebase couldn't support the feature reliably. I **rebuilt the entire game logic from scratch**, restructuring it for maintainability, extensibility, and real-time networking.

Key contributions:

- Rewrote core gameplay systems for clean architecture and extensibility
- Developed the matchmaking and lobby system
- Implemented real-time multiplayer networking

Available on Google Play (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=br.com.delotech.trucoindie)

---

### VR Racing Game

A **VR racing experience** developed for a Brazilian motorized wheelchair company. The game was showcased at **Rock in Rio 2017**, one of the world's largest music festivals, providing an accessible and immersive racing experience to attendees.

Key contributions:

- Customized Unity's vehicle physics system for wheelchair-based controls
- Implemented multiplayer racing for competitive on-site play
- Designed levels and tuned the driving experience

---

### VR Basketball

**VR Basketball** is an immersive VR experience built in **Unreal Engine 5 with C++** for **Meta Quest 2**. Gameplay systems and VR interactions are implemented entirely in C++ for maximum performance on standalone hardware.

Key highlights:

- Built with Unreal Engine 5 targeting Meta Quest 2
- Gameplay and interaction systems implemented in C++
- Physics-based ball handling and throwing mechanics
- Optimized for standalone headset performance

---

### Fox Trot

**Fox Trot** is a Web3 endless runner where players can purchase NFTs to customize their character. Built for WebGL with a focus on performance and extensibility.

Key contributions:

- Optimized the project for smooth WebGL performance
- Developed the core endless runner system
- Created extensible obstacle, collectible, and daily reward systems

Playable in the browser on itch.io (https://luizcarlosfx.itch.io/foxtrot)

---

### Mercenary Rush

**Mercenary Rush** is a 2D multiplayer platformer developed as a capstone project during my Computer Science degree. The project was a deep dive into real-time networking, Unity's Mecanim animation system, and custom editor tooling.

Key contributions:

- Built a responsive 2D platformer character controller
- Implemented real-time networking for fast-paced multiplayer gameplay
- Created custom editor tools (inspectors, windows, menus) for rapid iteration

---

### Donkey Kong 2 — Clone

A love letter to my childhood favorite — a faithful recreation of **Donkey Kong** built in Unity. With no dedicated artist on the team, I collected sprites from community resources and built custom editor tools to streamline the asset pipeline.

Watch the development playlist on YouTube (https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLedBjvIQnDOFqD_1fc-kaYi6rJwkAHIXX)

Key contributions:

- Built custom editor tools for sprite cutting and positioning from raw spritesheets
- Developed a 2D character controller with dual playable characters
- Created collectible, damage, and NPC systems

---

### Rick Dangerous — Remake

A remake of the classic arcade game **Rick Dangerous**, developed as a mentorship-driven project where I guided a team of junior artists and developers through the full game development process.

Key contributions:

- Led and mentored a team of junior developers and artists
- Established workflows and taught fundamental game development skills
- Built the character system, trap mechanics, and NPC behaviors
- Created reusable prefabs and tilemaps for level designers

Playable in the browser on itch.io (https://luizcarlosfx.itch.io/rick-dangerous)

---

### Pivot Editor

**Pivot Editor** is a Unity Editor extension published on the Unity Asset Store that allows developers to visually edit mesh pivot points directly in the editor using intuitive handle-based controls.

Key contributions:

- Designed and built the custom Editor Window
- Implemented pivot manipulation logic with visual handles
- Published and maintained the asset on the Unity Asset Store

Available on the Unity Asset Store (http://u3d.as/kT1)

---

### Sanja Runner

**Sanja Runner** is an educational 2D adventure game developed in 2016 for the city of São José dos Campos, Brazil. Players run through five levels based on real tourist landmarks — from Parque do Banhado to Parque Vicentina Aranha — collecting scrolls with educational content and answering quizzes along the way.

Built for PC and Android, the game features a content management system that allows educators to customize scrolls and quiz questions through a web interface — making it adaptable for different subjects and learning goals.

---

### Hyundai — VR Archery

A **VR archery game** developed for **Hyundai** as a brand activation at the 2017 Auto Show (*Feira do Automóvel*). Attendees could step into VR and experience an immersive archery challenge at the Hyundai booth.

Key contributions:

- Full development of the VR game
- Archery mechanics and physics for VR controllers
- Event-ready experience optimized for booth demonstrations

---
